title:
Module 3
SFW Image Creation
subtitle: Learn How to Generate SFW Images from Reference Images

blocks.content: Once you have your base model from Module 2, every new image you create should be generated using that base as a reference. This is how you maintain consistency across hundreds of images. The reference image workflow is the backbone of your content pipeline.

items: Start with your locked-in seed image from Module 2
Upload it as a reference/input image in your generation tool
Write a new prompt describing the desired scene, outfit, or setting
Generate multiple outputs and compare against the reference
Only keep images that match the face consistently

left.title: IMAGE PLACEHOLDER
left.content: Step-by-step screenshot of uploading reference image and generating a new variation

items: Use a clear, front-facing headshot as your primary reference
Keep 2-3 reference angles (front, 3/4 turn, profile) for variety
Higher resolution reference = better consistency in outputs
Never change your reference image once your page is live

right.title: IMAGE PLACEHOLDER
right.content: Example showing reference image → 3-4 different output images generated from it

sections.header: Lesson 2: Prompt Engineering for SFW Images

blocks.content: Your prompt is everything. The same reference image with a bad prompt produces unusable results. A great prompt produces content that looks like a real photoshoot. This lesson covers how to write prompts that consistently produce natural, platform-safe images.

items: Subject description (already handled by reference image)
Setting/background (indoor, outdoor, studio, café, etc.)
Outfit/clothing description
Lighting and mood (natural light, golden hour, soft studio)
Camera angle and framing (selfie, portrait, full body)
Style keywords (photorealistic, candid, editorial)

left.title: IMAGE PLACEHOLDER
left.content: Annotated prompt example showing each component highlighted

right.content: Prompt: 'Young woman in a white sundress sitting at an outdoor café, golden hour lighting, candid pose, looking at camera with soft smile, iPhone selfie style, photorealistic'

Prompt: 'Beautiful girl photo'
(Too vague — no setting, lighting, or style direction. Results will be random and inconsistent.)

right.title: IMAGE PLACEHOLDER
right.content: Side-by-side comparison: output from a good prompt vs output from a vague prompt

blocks.title: Pro Tip
blocks.content: Keep a prompt template document. When you find a prompt structure that works well, save it. Swap out the setting and outfit details for each new batch. This saves time and maintains quality.

Raw AI outputs are rarely perfect. Even the best generations need minor editing before they are ready to post. This lesson covers what to look for and how to fix common issues without over-processing the image.

items: Extra or distorted fingers/hands
Asymmetric facial features
Text or watermark artifacts in background
Unnatural skin smoothing or shine
Weird jewelry, accessories, or clothing seams
Background inconsistencies or blending errors

left.title: IMAGE PLACEHOLDER
left.content: Before/after examples of common AI artifacts being fixed

items: Inspect hands and fingers first (most common issue)
Check face symmetry and eye alignment
Scan background for text, artifacts, or impossible geometry
Adjust color grading for natural skin tones
Crop or reframe for platform-specific aspect ratios
Export at appropriate resolution

right.title: IMAGE PLACEHOLDER
right.content: Screenshot of editing workflow in photo editing tool

blocks.title: Rule
blocks.content: Do not over-edit. The goal is to fix obvious AI artifacts, not to create a heavily processed image. Over-edited photos look fake and reduce engagement.

Each platform has different content guidelines. What works on Reddit will get you banned on Instagram. Understanding what is 'safe' per platform prevents account strikes and keeps your content flowing without interruption.

items: No nudity, implied or explicit
No underwear as primary clothing
Cleavage must be modest and natural
Swimwear is allowed in context (beach, pool)
No sexual poses or suggestive hand placement
Similar rules to Instagram, often stricter
Carousels and photo content are safest
Avoid anything that could be flagged as 'adult content'
When in doubt, keep it casual and clothed
Varies by subreddit rules
SFW subreddits still require tasteful content
Read each subreddit's rules before posting
Some SFW subs allow swimwear, others don't
More lenient than Instagram
SFW content performs better for discovery
Mark sensitive content appropriately
Keep main feed clean, use media for variety

right.title: IMAGE PLACEHOLDER
right.content: Content tier chart: what's safe for each platform (green/yellow/red zones)

blocks.title: Golden Rule
blocks.content: When in doubt, keep it safe. One content strike on Instagram can shadow your account for weeks. It is always better to post something slightly too modest than to risk a ban.

summary_points: Always generate new images using your base model as a reference
Write detailed prompts with setting, lighting, outfit, and camera angle
Save prompt templates that produce consistently good results
Edit AI outputs to fix hands, artifacts, and background issues — but don't over-edit
Know each platform's content rules — Instagram is strictest, Reddit is most flexible
When in doubt, keep it safe — a content strike can derail weeks of progress
